perturbation.original_text: Oxbow and Global Energy hereby designate one another as their preferred suppliers of certain goods and services, as follows: (i) Oxbow shall be the preferred petroleum coke supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy. (ii) Oxbow shall be a preferred supplier of coal, coal fines, gob or waste coal products (collectively, "Coal") to gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy. (iii) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification technology supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects majority owned or controlled by Oxbow. (iv) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification project operator for petroleum coke gasification projects at sites majority owned or controlled by Oxbow.
perturbation.changed_text: Oxbow and Global Energy may designate one another as their preferred suppliers of certain goods and services, at their sole discretion, as follows: (i) Oxbow shall be the preferred petroleum coke supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy, unless otherwise determined by Global Energy. (ii) Oxbow shall be a preferred supplier of coal, coal fines, gob or waste coal products (collectively, "Coal") to gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy, unless otherwise determined by Global Energy. (iii) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification technology supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects majority owned or controlled by Oxbow, unless otherwise determined by Oxbow. (iv) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification project operator for petroleum coke gasification projects at sites majority owned or controlled by Oxbow, unless otherwise determined by Oxbow.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2(a) uses "hereby designate" and "shall be" to create a clear obligation for both parties to act as preferred suppliers. The modified text introduces uncertainty by using "may designate, at their sole discretion" and adding "unless otherwise determined by [Party]". This contradicts the original intent of establishing a guaranteed preferred supplier relationship and creates uncertainty about whether the parties are actually obligated to use each other's services. The use of "may" contradicts the immediately following statements where it states the parties "shall be" the preffered supplier, thus contradicting in the same location
perturbation.contradicted_text: Preferred Suppliers. Oxbow and Global Energy may designate one another as their preferred suppliers of certain goods and services, at their sole discretion, as follows: (i) Oxbow shall be the preferred petroleum coke supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy, unless otherwise determined by Global Energy. (ii) Oxbow shall be a preferred supplier of coal, coal fines, gob or waste coal products (collectively, "Coal") to gasification projects owned or controlled by Global Energy, unless otherwise determined by Global Energy. (iii) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification technology supplier to petroleum coke gasification projects majority owned or controlled by Oxbow, unless otherwise determined by Oxbow. (iv) Global Energy shall be the preferred gasification project operator for petroleum coke gasification projects at sites majority owned or controlled by Oxbow, unless otherwise determined by Oxbow.
perturbation.justification: The original text creates a clear obligation for both parties, while the modified text introduces uncertainty by using "may designate, at their sole discretion" and adding "unless otherwise determined by [Party]". The introduction of "may" and the ability for either party to unilaterally determine not to follow the preferred supplier arrangement directly contradicts the original intent and the following statements using "shall be", creating an in-text contradiction as per the provided explanation and Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: Global Energy agrees that Oxbow shall have a seat on Global Energy's Board of Directors.
perturbation.changed_text: Global Energy agrees that Oxbow may or may not have a seat on Global Energy's Board of Directors at Global Energy's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 7(g) states Oxbow "shall have a seat" on the Board if certain conditions are met creating a definitive obligation. The modified version replaces "shall" with "may or may not at Global Energy's sole discretion", negating any obligation and giving Global Energy complete control. This directly contradicts the earlier clause and creates uncertainty as to whether Oxbow is actually entitled to a board seat, even if it meets the ownership requirements. This creates uncertainty within the same location.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Board of Directors. During the term of this Agreement, and so long as Oxbow continues to own at least 15,000 common shares of Global Energy (as such amount may be adjusted to reflect any subsequent stock splits), Global Energy agrees that Oxbow may or may not have a seat on Global Energy's Board of Directors at Global Energy's sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, which replaces the definitive "shall have a seat" with the discretionary "may or may not have a seat at Global Energy's sole discretion", directly contradicts the original obligation in both the original text and contradicted text. The altered language introduces uncertainty and gives Global Energy unilateral control over Oxbow's board seat, even if Oxbow meets the ownership requirements stated. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it negates any obligation Global Energy has to provide Oxbow a board seat, and contradicts the implied entitlement in the contradicted text, making the clause unenforceable.
